items.claim: Damian Rebesky fights out of both orthodox and southpaw stances.
items.quote: Fights out of either stance. We've seen him both orthodox and southpaw, no problem.

items.claim: Damian Rebesky has good takedown defense using his hips to sprawl.
items.quote: He's got good take down defense. Gets sprawl. Um uses, you know, uses like a strong like hip to block the uh the takedown.

items.claim: Wellington Turman has a good cage push and can secure takedowns against the cage.
items.quote: He's got a good cage push. He can get his takedowns up against the cage.

items.claim: Islam Dulatov rushes in with a barrage of strikes sometimes.
items.quote: he does rush in sometimes with a barrage.

items.claim: Tyrell Fortune's best path to winning is his knee to the body.
items.quote: He's got a good knee to the body. That knee to the body is probably his best path to winning.

items.claim: Rizvan Kuniev has good takedown defense.
items.quote: solid grappler though, good takedown defense

items.claim: Steve Erceg has poor durability and has been hurt or knocked out in previous fights.
items.quote: durability though, that's the problem. I get nervous watching him fight if I've picked him to win. And that's because we've seen him really hurt in a lot of fights and knock out in fights as well.

items.claim: Bogdan Guskov is taking the fight against Magomed Ankalaev on short notice.
items.quote: Guskov's taking this fight on I you know, probably week and a half, two weeks notice.
he doesn't really have a bunch of head movement, so that could be a problem for him.

items.claim: Magomed Ankalaev can be too patient and is willing to win by landing minimal strikes.
items.quote: he can be too patient though. He's willing to just win a fight however, even if it means not doing anything but landing a couple little pokes and prods.
